definition: The simultaneous presence of basis trade concentration ($800B-$1T) and CLO arbitrage dependence creates unprecedented amplification risk, where a MOVE spike to 140+ simultaneously triggers basis trade margin calls and widens CLO AAA spreads past the SOFR+200 arbitrage-killing threshold.
evidence: March 2020 partial support: basis trade unwinds of ~$200-300B when MOVE hit 160+
$800B-$1T basis trade at 50-100x leverage is documented
CLO AAA spread threshold at SOFR+200 for arbitrage viability
BoJ-maturity wall timing overlap: Japanese holders of 20-30% CLO AAA tranches potentially repatriating
Simultaneous activation of both channels has no historical precedent at current scale
open_questions: What is the precise MOVE trigger level for basis trade margin calls?
Has post-2023 regulatory attention reduced basis trade concentration?
What is the joint probability of simultaneous channel activation (conjunction fallacy concern)?
Does the hedged-vs-directional split in vol-selling complex affect the cascade mechanism?
